World.headline: US tightens green card process, requires applicants to apply from abroad
World.detail: The US government has announced a new rule requiring most green card applicants to return to their home countries to apply, potentially impacting many currently residing in the United States. This change could cause disruptions for individuals and families awaiting permanent residency.

World.headline: Israel kills 10 people in Lebanon, including rescuers and a child in a 'double tap' strike
World.detail: An Israeli strike in Lebanon resulted in the deaths of 10 individuals, including rescue workers and a child, raising concerns about the targeting of civilians. The incident has sparked international condemnation and heightened tensions in the region.

An Air France flight en route to Detroit was diverted to Montreal due to concerns about potential Ebola exposure. Health officials are taking precautions, including screening at airports, following recent Ebola outbreaks.
World.headline: Air France flight to Detroit diverted to Montreal over Ebola fears
Rubio reports 'slight progress' in Iran talks as Pakistan renews efforts to mediate a peace deal
World.detail: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated that "slight progress" has been made during talks with Iran, while Pakistan is increasing its efforts to mediate a peace agreement. The diplomatic activity aims to de-escalate tensions and find a resolution to the ongoing conflict.

Tulsi Gabbard has resigned from her position as Director of US National Intelligence. Gabbard cited her husband's cancer diagnosis as a contributing factor, though reports suggest disagreements with the administration on Iran also played a role.
World.headline: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Director of US National Intelligence

Health.headline: Suspected Ebola cases triple in a week as WHO warns of rapid spread in DRC
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued a warning about the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), with suspected cases tripling in a week. The WHO is considering the use of experimental vaccines as cases and deaths rise.

Health.headline: U.S. bans green-card holders from returning from Ebola-stricken countries
Health.detail: Citing public health and "resource constraints," U.S. health officials have expanded travel restrictions, banning green-card holders from returning from countries affected by Ebola. DHS rules now require individuals who have recently visited certain African countries to enter the U.S. through Dulles International Airport.
Researchers have identified cancer-causing chemicals present in common foods. This discovery highlights the need for greater awareness and potential regulation of these chemicals in the food supply.
Health.headline: Scientists Uncover Cancer-causing Chemicals Hidden in Everyday Foods
Higher Caffeine Intake Tied to Greater Depression Severity
Health.detail: According to a recent study presented at APA 2026, increased caffeine consumption is linked to more severe symptoms of depression. The research suggests a potential correlation between caffeine intake and mental health.

The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo a global health emergency. An Ebola outbreak caused by the rare Bundibugyo strain has killed dozens in Democratic Republic of the Congo.
Health.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC a global health emergency

Politics.headline: Denmark's centre-right coalition talks break down, prolonging political deadlock
Politics.detail: Denmark's acting Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen's attempt to form a centre-right government broke down, prolonging a post-election stalemate in the Nordic country's parliament after failing to secure support from the centrist Moderate Party. King Frederik will now hold fresh talks with party leaders to decide the next steps.

President Trump's recent decision to deploy 5,000 troops to Poland has created confusion among European NATO partners, leaving them uncertain about which areas they should prioritize. This erratic policy move has deepened uncertainty regarding US commitments in Europe.
Politics.headline: Trump's About-Face on U.S. Troops in Poland Mystifies Allies
Talks Still Ongoing on Points of Disagreement, Source Says
Politics.detail: A source close to the Iranian negotiating team told Tasnim that talks and consultations are still underway regarding disagreements on how to end the war against Iran. A Pakistani mediator is facilitating discussions between both sides, with the primary focus on finalizing the terms for ending the conflict.

Politics.headline: Judge drops criminal case against Kilmar Abrego García, deeming it vindictive
Politics.detail: A judge dismissed the human smuggling charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was previously deported to El Salvador, citing the federal prosecution as vindictive. The case was dropped after a high-profile legal battle where courts ruled his deportation had been illegal.

Politics.headline: NATO Chief Rutte Discusses Russia, Iran and Ukraine After FMs Meeting in Sweden
Politics.detail: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte stated that the alliance is growing stronger after high-level discussions in Helsingborg, Sweden, addressing Russia, Ukraine, defense spending, Iran, and freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Rutte emphasized the importance of countries ensuring the Strait of Hormuz remains open for transit, including by deploying essential assets to the region.

Finance & Economy.headline: Falling drone debris triggers fire at oil terminal in Russia's Novorossiysk
Finance & Economy.detail: Reports indicate that falling drone debris caused a fire at an oil terminal in Novorossiysk, Russia.

Finance & Economy.headline: Europe stocks lose sparkle
Finance & Economy.detail: A market rotation from expensive US stocks into cheaper European names has been derailed by the global energy shock tied to the Middle East conflict and the obsession with artificial intelligence.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK pitched single market for goods with EU
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK has proposed a single market for goods with the EU.

The Malaysian Ringgit ended its three-day rally against the US dollar due to ongoing US-Iran negotiations and central banks adopting hawkish stances; Malaysia's trade performance in April 2026 saw a 28.6% year-on-year increase to RM336.73 billion, driven by strong E&E exports.
Finance & Economy.headline: Ringgit ends three-day rally against US dollar amid geopolitical concerns
NANOBIOTIX Announces Full Exercise of Underwriters' Over-Allotment Option, Bringing Gross Proceeds of Offering to Approximately $100 Million
Finance & Economy.detail: NANOBIOTIX, a biotechnology company, announced that the underwriters of its global offering have fully exercised their option to purchase additional new ordinary shares, bringing the gross proceeds to approximately $100 million. The company believes these proceeds, along with existing cash, will cover working capital needs into 2029.

Zimbabwe.detail: Police in Zimbabwe are conducting a crackdown on knife crime following a surge in violent attacks involving knives.
Zimbabwe.headline: Police crackdown on knife crime as violent attacks surge

Zimbabwe.detail: Harare is experiencing a wave of vandalism targeting prepaid water meters, disrupting water access for residents.
Zimbabwe.headline: Wave of vandalism hits Harare's prepaid water meters
Commercial milk production surges
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has seen a surge in commercial milk production, potentially boosting the country's dairy industry.
Namib is making progress in restarting the Redwing Mine, with dewatering efforts advancing.
Zimbabwe.headline: Namib advances Redwing Mine restart as dewatering progresses

Zimbabwe.detail: Twenty schools in Harare have received Starlink connectivity kits, which aim to improve internet access for students and educators.
Zimbabwe.headline: 20 Harare schools receive Starlink connectivity kits

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Britain's coming energy shock: mini-measures won't suffice
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discussing the insufficient measures being taken to address the impending energy crisis in Britain.
This editorial celebrates the success and importance of gardens in galleries and museums.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on gallery and museum gardens: a blooming triumph

Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discussing the misalignment of interests between India and the US in the current global scenario and the need to address this divergence.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Pratap Bhanu Mehta. Today, India and US interests are not easily aligned. Let's confront this.

Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post's editorial board discusses how China's tight control on rare earth elements is now causing market reactions.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial Board. China squeezed too tight on rare earths. Now the market is taking action.
Editorial Board. A warning from New Zealand.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post's editorial board issues a warning based on events or policies in New Zealand.

Education.headline: More than 160,000 SPM school leavers offered IPTA spots
Education.detail: A total of 169,803 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 school leavers have been offered places at public higher learning institutions (IPTA), representing 72.11% of the total 235,480 applications received.

Education.headline: Rural Schools Face Widening Education Gap Under Korea's High School Credit System
Education.detail: Small rural high schools in Korea are facing limitations in course choices and teacher shortages due to low student enrollment, raising concerns about a widening education gap two years into the high school credit system rollout. Schools in North Gyeongsang (Gyeongbuk) and South Jeolla (Jeonnam) provinces are particularly affected, offering fewer courses and employing fewer teachers compared to regular high schools.
British Council staff in Italy are planning a strike in response to a proposed 80% reduction in the workforce.
Education.headline: British Council staff in Italy to strike over proposed 80% workforce cut.
Miami-Dade County considers 9 school closures, consolidations.
Education.detail: The Florida district saw a 4% decrease in enrollment between the 2024-25 and 2025-26 school years — driven largely by a decline in newcomer students and is considering closing schools as a result.

Teachers experience poor work-life balance compared to similar working adults, according to a RAND survey of teachers, released in October and funded in part by NEA. Three times as many teachers as similar working adults said it was difficult to change their work schedule or attend to personal or family matters while on the job.
Education.headline: Teachers Need More Work-Life Balance

Entertainment.detail: Tom Hardy has reportedly been fired from MobLand after season two due to tensions behind the scenes. The show will continue without him.
Entertainment.headline: Tom Hardy fired from MobLand as tensions emerge behind the scenes: Report
'Dragon' First Glimpse Out: NTR Unleashes Fury In KGF Maker Prashanth Neel's Pan-India Opium War Drama
Entertainment.detail: Jr NTR's 'Dragon', directed by Prashanth Neel, reveals a first look into a fierce opium war drama. The pan-India film is set to release on June 11 next year and features a huge ensemble cast.

Ricky Martin's concert in Montenegro was halted when someone released suspected tear gas or pepper spray into the crowd, causing fans to seek safety and medical attention. Martin resumed the show after the area was cleared and deemed safe.
Entertainment.headline: Ricky Martin safe after 'tear gas' — or maybe pepper spray? — stops his concert in Montenegro

Entertainment.detail: Anne Hathaway addressed rumors of plastic surgery, denying she's had any major procedures. She mentioned a hairstyling trick that mimics a facelift effect and stated she might consider a facelift in the future.
Entertainment.headline: Anne Hathaway Shuts Down Plastic Surgery Rumors
Puig and The Estée Lauder end negotiations without a merger
Entertainment.detail: Puig Brands and The Estée Lauder Companies have ended merger negotiations. The merger would have created a cosmetics giant with a large market capitalization.

Crime & Justice.headline: Lawyers shot outside North Carolina courthouse
Crime & Justice.detail: In Raleigh, North Carolina, a woman's civil case turned into a criminal one after police said she shot two lawyers outside a courthouse, and both were hospitalized.
Aimee Bock has been sentenced to 500 months in prison for her lead role in a $250 million fraud scheme that exploited a federally funded child nutrition program.
Crime & Justice.headline: Feeding Our Future Ringleader Sentenced to 500 Months

Crime & Justice.detail: A Georgian citizen was sentenced to 37 months in prison for laundering more than $1.1 million in illicit health care fraud proceeds to his co-conspirators located abroad.
Crime & Justice.headline: Georgian Citizen Sentenced to Over Three Years in Prison for International Money Laundering Conspiracy
One of shooters in deadly attack at San Diego mosque was previously flagged by FBI
Crime & Justice.detail: Officials seized guns from Caleb Vazquez's father last year amid alarm over teen's views on mass shooters and Nazism.

Crime & Justice.headline: Jamal Yunos makes public apology to settle KJ's defamation suit
Crime & Justice.detail: Sungai Besar Umno chief Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos made a public apology at the High Court to settle a defamation suit filed by former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin, admitting that his allegations were untrue and without sufficient proof.

Technology.detail: The companies will jointly explore a new paradigm of livelihood services featuring “AI + Cloud + Computing Power Collaboration”, focusing on sectors like healthcare, employment, and government services. This collaboration aims to connect the full chain from computing infrastructure to scenario implementation, unlocking large-scale growth.
Technology.headline: TECSUN SCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CO.,LTD. Partners with QingCloud Technologies Corp. to Forge New Engine for AI-Powered Livelihood Services

Technology.detail: Cybersecurity company TrendLife reports that impersonation scams are dominating the threat landscape, with scammers posing as trusted entities to steal money and personal information. More than half a million scam attempts were detected across Australia and New Zealand in April alone.
Technology.headline: Cyber Scam Explosion Hits Aussies And Kiwis As Impersonation Fraud Surges
Spotify and Universal Music agree deal to let subscribers create AI remixes
Technology.detail: Spotify and Universal Music have reached an agreement that will allow subscribers to create AI remixes. This deal signifies a step forward in the integration of AI in music creation and consumption.

Technology.headline: Sodium Poised to Overtake Lithium in Battery Tech
Technology.detail: Researchers at the University of Limerick's Bernal Institute have produced a battery combining the strengths of sodium and lithium, potentially leading to more sustainable batteries. The new battery design addresses the shortcomings of sodium-ion batteries, offering a promising alternative to lithium-ion batteries.

Technology.headline: AMD to promote packaging ecosystem
Technology.detail: AMD is investing US$10 billion in Taiwan's microchip ecosystem to secure advanced packaging technology capacity over the next three years, driven by the growing demand for AI infrastructure. The investment aims to foster a new advanced packaging ecosystem in Taiwan using elevated fan-out bridge (EFB) technology.

Lifestyle.detail: Everlane, known for its ethically sourced and sustainable clothing, is being acquired by Shein, the fast-fashion powerhouse, in a move confirmed by Everlane's CEO Alfred Chang.
Lifestyle.headline: Shein Moves to Buy Eco-Conscious Label Everlane
Outstanding country lifestyle in sought-after Buckley
Lifestyle.detail: A lifestyle property in Buckley offers the very best of country living while keeping you close to the Surf Coast and Geelong, featuring a wide verandah and rural views.

Lifestyle.headline: Highlights and memorable moments from the red carpet fashion at Cannes 2026
Lifestyle.detail: The Cannes Film Festival is wrapping up its twelve days of premieres and high fashion, with the red carpet serving as a renowned fashion showcase.
This article provides a list of activities and events to look forward to between May 22nd and May 28th.
Lifestyle.headline: Guru’s Weekly Buzz: May 22-28

Lifestyle.detail: Chemicals in everyday plastics can interfere with our hormones and while no direct link to fertility has been established, they have been associated with issues such as polycystic ovary syndrome and poorer egg quality.
Lifestyle.headline: From takeaway containers to receipts – how everyday plastics may disrupt hormones and your fertility

Science.detail: Researchers have discovered the atomic structure of a protective surface layer on gold that prevents it from rusting or corroding, revealing why this precious metal remains lustrous.
Science.headline: Scientists Find The Atomic Reason That Gold Refuses to Rust
SpaceX launches its biggest rocket yet in test flight from Texas
Science.detail: SpaceX has launched its biggest, most powerful Starship yet from South Texas, carrying 20 mock Starlink satellites on a test flight extending halfway around the world.

Scientists have identified potentially cancer-causing chemicals hiding in many everyday foods, especially those exposed to high heat cooking methods like grilling, roasting, smoking, and frying. The compounds, known as PAHs, can form during cooking.
Science.headline: Scientists Uncover Cancer-causing Chemicals Hidden in Everyday Foods

Science.detail: A recent article explains the physics behind why gold remains pristine. The atomic structure of a protective surface layer on gold prevents it from rusting or corroding.
Science.headline: Physics explains why gold stays pristine

Science.detail: Congo was prepared for Ebola, but now a rare strain is exposing gaps in readiness. The article discusses the challenges of containing the outbreak.
Science.headline: Congo prepared for Ebola. Now a rare strain is exposing gaps in readiness

Sports.detail: RCB captain Rajat Patidar acknowledged that Sunrisers Hyderabad's score of 255 was too challenging for Royal Challengers Bangalore to chase in their recent match.
Sports.headline: Rajat Patidar admits SRH's 255 was 'too much' for RCB in Hyderabad clash.
RCB captain Rajat Patidar overtakes Chris Gayle in six-hitting feat.
Sports.detail: Rajat Patidar surpassed Chris Gayle in a six-hitting record during a recent game.

Sports.headline: Rashee Rice headed to jail for 30 days: Will the NFL suspend him again?
Sports.detail: NFL player Rashee Rice is reportedly facing a 30-day jail sentence, raising questions about a potential further suspension from the league.

Sports.headline: Congo World Cup squad must isolate before entry to US: official.
Sports.detail: An official has stated that the Congo World Cup team is required to isolate before being allowed to enter the United States.

Sports.headline: Former Wisconsin guard John Blackwell withdraws from NBA draft, will play at Duke next season.
Sports.detail: John Blackwell, previously a guard for Wisconsin, has withdrawn his name from the NBA draft and will be playing basketball at Duke University in the upcoming season.
